name: sj-core-catalog
description: Choose the right Steve Jobs operating skill and sequence. Use when the user asks for a Steve Jobs lens, Jobs-style critique, simplification, product craft, launch thinking, hiring bar, failure review, or does not know which sj skill to use.

name: sj-core-compound-learning
description: Capture 1-3 approved Jobs-derived lessons into a durable project learning store. Use after a product review, launch, hiring decision, strategy session, failure autopsy, or skill chain produced reusable operating knowledge.

name: sj-core-learning-refresh
description: Audit saved Steve Jobs learning notes for stale, duplicate, generic, contradictory, or low-confidence guidance. Use when maintaining docs/steve-jobs/learnings/ or preparing SJ learnings for reuse.

name: sj-product-make-something-wonderful
description: Raise ambition, care, usefulness, and emotional quality. Use when a product, app, feature, artifact, essay, or plan is technically adequate but needs more taste, care, humanity, or wonder.

name: sj-product-customer-backwards
description: Start from the user experience and work back to technology. Use when a proposal starts with technology, implementation, model choice, architecture, or features before naming the customer experience.

name: sj-product-bicycle-for-the-mind
description: Frame tools as human leverage rather than machinery. Use when explaining a tool, computer, AI system, app, or workflow as an amplifier of human ability.

name: sj-product-liberal-arts-technology
description: Connect engineering with taste, story, culture, and emotion. Use when a product feels technically competent but culturally thin, emotionally flat, or disconnected from human meaning.

name: sj-product-speaks-for-itself
description: Remove design that requires explanation to make sense. Use when a UI, onboarding, demo, flow, or feature depends on presenter explanation, docs, tooltips, or apologetic copy.

name: sj-product-design-is-how-it-works
description: Judge design by behavior, not surface styling. Use when reviewing UI, UX, product design, service design, or architecture where function and experience matter more than decoration.

name: sj-product-simplify-to-one
description: Reduce a product, path, message, or priority set to one clear thing. Use when something feels bloated, over-explained, committee-shaped, split across too many messages, features, users, paths, or owners.

name: sj-product-simple-stick
description: Challenge undistilled, winding, or overcomplicated thinking. Use when a proposal, interface, explanation, or plan rambles, hides weak thinking in complexity, or needs sharper directness.

name: sj-product-hack-away-unessential
description: Remove anything that does not serve the core experience. Use when a feature list, workflow, roadmap, or design needs pruning, deferral, merging, or sharper tradeoffs.

name: sj-product-speed-as-feature
description: Choose speed or responsiveness as a product rule. Use when performance, latency, waiting, loading, build speed, or workflow drag may be the defining product experience.

name: sj-product-apple-experience-audit
description: Audit every touchpoint as a trust deposit or withdrawal. Use when reviewing customer support, onboarding, purchase, install, trial, docs, error states, packaging, retail, or any end-to-end experience.

name: sj-product-back-of-cabinet-quality
description: Care about hidden quality even when users may not see it. Use when evaluating code quality, architecture, invisible UX, manufacturing, internal tools, naming, docs, or polish behind the surface.

name: sj-product-taste-review
description: Use judgment where metrics and committees are not enough. Use when a design, name, interface, launch, or product choice needs taste, restraint, and coherent judgment rather than only data.

name: sj-product-pixel-polish
description: Review the artifact at detail level and improve finish. Use when a visual interface, presentation, landing page, demo, or artifact needs detailed polish and exactness.

name: sj-product-no-second-rate-products
description: Identify mediocrity and force a higher standard. Use when a product line, feature, design, or plan has acceptable-but-not-great parts that should be cut, remade, or clarified.

name: sj-product-beautiful-object-standard
description: Ask whether the thing deserves to exist as an object. Use when judging hardware, apps, documents, interfaces, tools, names, or experiences as crafted objects people should want to use.

name: sj-product-telegraph-to-telephone
description: Turn expert complexity into everyday usability. Use when simplifying technical, command-line, AI, developer, enterprise, or professional tools for ordinary users.

name: sj-product-founder-workbench
description: Demystify reality by making and inspecting things directly. Use when a founder, builder, or agent is too abstract and needs to touch the materials, prototype, code, customer flow, or real artifact.

name: sj-product-reputation-credits
description: Treat each interaction as adding or withdrawing trust. Use when auditing quality, support, onboarding, performance, reliability, messaging, or any customer contact point.

name: sj-product-skepticism-hierarchy
description: Sort objections by signal, source, and importance. Use when feedback conflicts, critics disagree, or a product team needs to know which objections matter.

name: sj-product-raw-work-no-filters
description: Get decision makers close to the actual artifact. Use when layers, summaries, decks, managers, or process prevent leaders from seeing the real product, code, design, or customer issue.

name: sj-product-creative-selection
description: Run a concrete demo-driven iteration loop. Use when a product, feature, interface, prototype, pitch, or design needs artifact-based review instead of abstract discussion.

name: sj-product-concrete-artifact-review
description: Force decisions around visible work rather than ideas. Use when the conversation is stuck in hypotheticals and needs a screen, draft, prototype, patch, diagram, or demo.

name: sj-ive-design-story
description: Find the product's design story before debating features. Use when an artifact feels like disconnected features, lacks meaning, or needs a human metaphor for how it enters daily life.

name: sj-ive-humanize-technology
description: Make technology feel approachable, friendly, touchable, and emotionally legible. Use when a tool feels cold, intimidating, abstract, or too technical for normal users.

name: sj-ive-first-touch-moment
description: Audit the first thing a user sees, opens, touches, hears, wears, or feels. Use when onboarding, packaging, first screen, or first physical interaction must earn trust.

name: sj-ive-prototype-volume
description: Replace argument with many concrete models and selection. Use when a team is debating taste, form, interaction, or direction without enough prototypes to compare.

name: sj-ive-material-honesty
description: Review material, finish, weight, color, sound, edge, and texture as part of the idea. Use when an artifact's physical or sensory qualities affect trust and desire.

name: sj-ive-manufacturing-as-design
description: Treat production, tooling, suppliers, and implementation constraints as part of design. Use when a concept may not survive manufacturing or engineering reality.

name: sj-ive-care-is-felt
description: Audit whether users can sense care or carelessness in an artifact. Use when something works functionally but feels indifferent, rough, cheap, or unloved.

name: sj-ive-better-not-different
description: Test whether novelty makes the product better, not merely different. Use when a design, feature, or brand move is unusual but its improvement is unclear.

name: sj-ive-fragile-idea-protection
description: Protect early promising ideas from premature committee, metric, or engineering flattening. Use when a rough concept needs room to mature before judgment.

name: sj-ive-future-without-betrayal
description: Move a beloved product, brand, or category into the future without losing its soul. Use for redesigns, AI shifts, electrification, or major technology transitions.

name: sj-story-one-message-marketing
description: Make one clear promise across copy, ads, and launch. Use when marketing has too many messages, audiences, claims, taglines, benefits, or explanations.

name: sj-story-sell-the-improvement
description: Sell the better life, not the product feature. Use when converting feature lists, specs, technical claims, or product details into customer-facing value.

name: sj-story-three-act-launch
description: Build the problem, meaning, solution arc. Use when writing a keynote, demo, launch post, sales page, pitch, video script, or investor/product narrative.

name: sj-story-number-context
description: Make numbers vivid and meaningful. Use when metrics, specs, performance claims, market sizes, pricing, or comparisons need context people can feel.

name: sj-story-historical-analogy
description: Use history to make the future understandable. Use when explaining a new technology, market shift, tool, platform, or product category through a familiar analogy.

name: sj-story-demo-rehearsal-loop
description: Practice until the demo feels inevitable. Use when preparing a keynote, launch, investor demo, product walkthrough, screencast, or live presentation.

name: sj-story-values-marketing
description: Explain what the brand stands for. Use when brand messaging should communicate values, identity, and belief rather than only product facts.

name: sj-story-missionary-pr
description: Use publicity to educate, not just hype. Use when drafting PR, press strategy, launch education, media outreach, or founder messaging around a new category.

name: sj-story-old-story-new-tool
description: Put new technology inside enduring human stories. Use when a product story feels too technical and needs timeless human stakes, characters, tension, or transformation.

name: sj-people-a-player-bar
description: Apply an unusually high talent standard. Use when hiring, evaluating collaborators, designing a team, raising standards, or deciding whether someone improves the room.

name: sj-people-missionaries-not-mercenaries
description: Filter for purpose, ownership, and belief. Use when judging candidates, cofounders, agencies, vendors, teammates, or partners for mission fit versus transactional motivation.

name: sj-people-pushback-interview
description: Test independent thinking and intelligent disagreement. Use when designing interviews, collaboration filters, team reviews, or founder conversations that need real pushback.

name: sj-people-small-a-team
description: Prefer a small excellent team over a large average one. Use when staffing a project, cutting coordination drag, simplifying ownership, or comparing team designs.

name: sj-people-one-owner-no-committees
description: Assign clear ownership and remove committee drift. Use when decisions diffuse across groups, committees, review boards, or unclear owners.

name: sj-people-blunt-truth-review
description: Say the direct thing clearly and usefully. Use when feedback, strategy, copy, or review needs directness without euphemism, fog, or false politeness.

name: sj-people-no-seagull-management
description: Avoid detached executive drive-by feedback. Use when leaders parachute into work late, create chaos, or give shallow critiques without context.

name: sj-people-management-by-values
description: Align destination and values, then allow flexible routes. Use when a team needs principles, decision rules, or operating values more than micromanaged instructions.

name: sj-people-ideas-from-anywhere
description: Remove hierarchy from idea flow. Use when good ideas may be blocked by title, function, seniority, process, or fear of speaking up.

name: sj-people-founder-soul-guardian
description: Protect the product or company soul. Use when growth, funding, bureaucracy, acquisition, scale, or short-term pressure threatens the core identity.

name: sj-people-ceo-at-bottom
description: Treat leadership as service to the builders. Use when leadership needs humility, talent retention, clearer service posture, or less command-and-control energy.

name: sj-people-confidant-board
description: Use trusted outsiders to organize thought. Use when a founder, builder, or operator needs serious sounding boards, coaches, confidants, or mental organization.

name: sj-people-ask-for-help
description: Ask serious builders directly and specifically. Use when seeking mentorship, advice, introductions, customer conversations, expert review, or help from people ahead of you.

name: sj-people-smart-people-can-leave
description: Remember talented people are volunteers, not possessions. Use when reviewing retention, leadership behavior, autonomy, team culture, or founder arrogance around talent.

name: sj-people-permanent-ensemble
description: Build a recurring creative team with compounding trust. Use when designing a studio, product org, creative team, research group, or agent team intended to improve together over time.

name: sj-people-truth-to-founder
description: Create channels for reality to reach the decision maker. Use when leaders are insulated, teams hide bad news, metrics are massaged, or reality is not reaching the person with authority.

name: sj-people-ed-catmull-patience
description: Use facts, trust, and time to change strong minds. Use when persuading a difficult but intelligent leader, founder, or stakeholder without escalating into ego conflict.

name: sj-people-andy-grove-correction
description: Accept hard correction from a serious operator. Use when feedback from a strong operator conflicts with pride, attachment, or your current strategy.

name: sj-people-bob-iger-trust
description: Negotiate with transparency when trust matters. Use when a negotiation, partnership, acquisition, or leadership relationship depends on trust more than tactical dominance.

name: sj-strategy-focus-matrix
description: Reduce product lines, priorities, or bets to a clear grid. Use when strategy, roadmap, product lines, offerings, or priorities are scattered and need focus.

name: sj-strategy-differentiation-or-death
description: Check whether the product is meaningfully different. Use when a product risks becoming a commodity, copycat, feature checklist, or price-only competitor.

name: sj-strategy-innovate-out
description: Respond to commoditization with invention. Use when differentiation is evaporating and the answer must be a new product, category, architecture, or experience.

name: sj-strategy-direct-channel
description: Build a direct relationship with customers. Use when strategy depends on distribution, web, community, retail, content, support, or owning the customer relationship.

name: sj-strategy-ambush-distribution
description: Meet customers where attention and traffic already are. Use when a product needs a retail, web, content, community, partnership, or launch distribution strategy.

name: sj-strategy-say-yes-then-learn
description: Commit before full certainty when the risk is survivable. Use when deciding whether to accept an opportunity, ship a stretch promise, learn under pressure, or take a calculated leap.

name: sj-strategy-reality-is-malleable
description: Challenge learned helplessness and false constraints. Use when a plan is blocked by assumptions, inherited limits, fear, or claims that something cannot be changed.

name: sj-strategy-failure-apprenticeship
description: Turn failure into operating wisdom without flattering it. Use when a project failed, a strategy is stuck, a founder is wounded, or a reset needs lessons rather than comfort.

name: sj-strategy-startup-discipline
description: Keep scarcity, focus, and truth inside the startup. Use when money, prestige, vanity, hiring, offices, manufacturing, or process may be making a young effort soft.

name: sj-strategy-pivot-to-core
description: Find the real business after the first plan fails. Use when the original product, market, or business model is not working but valuable technology or capability remains.

name: sj-strategy-synthesizer-strategy
description: Combine separate technologies into one coherent product. Use when many ingredients exist but need a product vision that synthesizes them into a new whole.

name: sj-strategy-do-not-overplay-hand
description: Use leverage with restraint. Use when negotiating, partnering, acquiring, fundraising, selling, or deciding how hard to push an advantage.

name: sj-strategy-build-for-yourself
description: Build what the team deeply wants and understands. Use when product taste may be outsourced to surveys, committees, or imagined users instead of lived need.

name: sj-strategy-company-as-invention
description: Treat the company as the machine that makes great products. Use when designing culture, operating cadence, hiring, values, product process, or long-term company capability.

name: sj-strategy-web-as-community
description: Use the web as relationship infrastructure. Use when designing direct-to-consumer channels, community, education, support, publishing, or customer feedback loops.

name: sj-learning-biographies-as-mentors
description: Use lives of builders as reusable mentorship. Use when choosing what to read, extracting lessons from biographies, or building a personal operating system from history.

name: sj-learning-regret-vs-mistake
description: Separate reversible mistakes from life regrets. Use when making major life, product, career, founder, or relationship decisions under uncertainty.

name: sj-learning-mortality-lens
description: Use finitude to clarify courage and priority. Use when a decision needs urgency, honesty, focus, or escape from status games and fear.

name: sj-learning-avocation-to-vocation
description: Turn deep curiosity into work. Use when exploring career direction, creative work, founder ideas, personal projects, or what to build next.

name: sj-learning-endurance-loop
description: Design the repeated effort needed for mastery. Use when a goal requires sustained iteration, pain tolerance, patience, or repeated attempts before quality appears.

name: sj-learning-fundamentals-practice
description: Return to the basic reps that compound. Use when improving craft, product work, writing, sales, design, coding, leadership, or any skill through repeated fundamentals.

name: sj-learning-progressive-summarization
description: Turn books, podcasts, and notes into reusable knowledge. Use when processing reading, transcripts, podcast notes, highlights, or source material into durable summaries and skills.

name: sj-learning-balanced-builder-audit
description: Check ambition against health, family, joy, and sanity. Use when intense work, obsession, founder drive, or excellence risks becoming too expensive personally.

name: sj-learning-all-glory-fleeting
description: Keep ego and success in perspective. Use when success, status, praise, winning, or recognition might distort judgment or priorities.

name: sj-anti-revenge-motive-check
description: Detect when strategy is secretly ego or revenge. Use when a project, company, negotiation, or response may be driven by proving someone wrong rather than serving the product or customer.

name: sj-anti-channel-stuffing-check
description: Separate real traction from fake comfort. Use when sales, usage, metrics, revenue, inventory, or pipeline might be inflated, pulled forward, or misread as demand.

name: sj-anti-too-much-money-check
description: Prevent abundant funding from killing discipline. Use when a team has money, attention, headcount, or prestige and may be losing startup urgency, truth, or focus.
